title: How the Tiger Got His Stripes

events.name: Tiger Seeks Farm Worker
events.description: Once upon a time, ages and ages ago, so long ago that the tiger had no stripes upon his back, and the rabbit still had his tail, there was a tiger who had a farm. The farm was very much overgrown with underbrush and the owner sought a workman to clear the ground for him to plant.
thoughts: The event is part of the main narrative action: it sets up a situation that will drive the story forward. The tiger's farm is in disarray and needs clearing, which implies conflict or challenge to be resolved.

The event describes a problem or situation that needs to be addressed, which drives the story forward. It's not an action taken by the hero, nor is it a resolution of conflict. This matches option 0 as it sets up the main narrative action.

events.name: Tiger Offers Work Reward
events.description: The tiger called all the beasts together and said to them when they had assembled, “I need a good workman at once to clear my farm of the underbrush. To the one of you who will do this work I offer an ox in payment.”
thoughts: The event involves the tiger calling all beasts together and offering a reward, which sets up the problem of clearing the underbrush. This is part of the main narrative action as it drives the story forward through preparation.

events.name: Tiger Rejects Monkey Worker
events.description: The monkey was the first one to step forward and apply for the position. The tiger tried him for a little while but he was not a good workman at all. He did not work steadily enough to accomplish anything. The tiger discharged him very soon and he did not pay him.
thoughts: The event involves the tiger acting on and reacting to a job applicant, which drives the story forward by introducing conflict and testing the hero's worthiness for the position.
The event involves a character (the monkey) being tested and rejected by another character (the tiger), which drives the story forward through conflict and preparation for the main plot.

events.name: Tiger Discharges Unreliable Goat
events.description: Then the tiger hired the goat to do the work. The goat worked faithfully enough but he did not have the brains to do the work well. He would clear a little of the farm in one place and then he would go away and work on another part of it. He never finished anything neatly. The tiger discharged him very soon without paying him.
thoughts: The goat's hiring by the tiger and subsequent dismissal is a continuation of the story's setup phase, where the tiger tries out different animals for farm work. This event is not part of the main conflict or plot progression.

The event involves the tiger acting upon a previous hire, discharging the goat for poor work and not paying him. This is part of the main narrative action as it drives the story forward by showing the tiger's continued struggle to find a suitable worker.

events.name: Armadillo Fails Farm Task
events.description: Next the tiger tried the armadillo. The armadillo was very strong and he did the work well. The trouble with him was that he had such a big appetite. There were a great many ants about the place and the armadillo could never pass by a sweet tender juicy ant without stopping to eat it.
thoughts: The armadillo's attempt to clear the farm is another test by the tiger to find a suitable workman. The armadillo shows promise but ultimately fails due to his insatiable appetite.

events.name: Tiger Selects New Candidate
events.description: The tiger laughed at him and said, “Why, little rabbit, you are too small to do the work. The monkey, the goat, and the armadillo have all failed to give satisfaction. Of course a little beast like you will fail too.” However, there were no other beasts who applied for the position, so the tiger sent for the rabbit and told him that he would try him for a little while.
thoughts: The event involves the tiger acting on previous events and selecting a new candidate for the job. This is part of the main narrative action as it drives the story forward through conflict and external help.

The event involves the hero acting within the core plot, as the rabbit applies for and begins working on the farm clearing task. This event drives the story forward by introducing a new character and escalating the conflict of finding a suitable workman.

events.name: Tiger Leaves for Hunting Trip
events.description: The rabbit worked faithfully and well, and soon he had cleared a large portion of the ground. The next day he worked just as well. The tiger thought that he had been very lucky to hire the rabbit. He got tired staying around to watch the rabbit work. The rabbit seemed to know just how to do the work anyway, without orders, so the tiger decided to go away on a hunting trip.
thoughts: The event shows the tiger's satisfaction with the rabbit's work and his decision to go away on a hunting trip, indicating that the initial conflict or problem has been resolved. The tiger no longer needs to supervise the rabbit, and the narrative context suggests that the rabbit has successfully cleared a large portion of the ground.

events.name: Rabbit Inspects Tiger's Gift
events.description: He left his son to watch the rabbit. After the tiger had gone away, the rabbit said to the tiger’s son, “The ox your father is going to give me is marked with a white spot on his left ear and another on his right side, isn’t he?” “O, no,” replied the tiger’s son.

events.description: “He is red all over with just a tiny white spot on his right ear.” The rabbit worked for a while longer and then he said, “The ox which your father is going to give me is kept by the river, isn’t he?” “Yes,” replied the tiger’s son.

events.name: Rabbit clears the farm.
events.description: The rabbit had made a plan to go and get the ox without waiting to finish his work. Just as he started off he saw the tiger returning. The tiger noticed that the rabbit had not worked so well when he was away. After that, he stayed and watched the rabbit until the whole farm was cleared.
thoughts: The rabbit makes a plan to go and get the ox without waiting to finish his work, but then sees the tiger returning. The tiger stays and watches the rabbit until the whole farm is cleared. This event represents a turning point in the narrative where the rabbit's success is confirmed, and the story takes a new direction.

The rabbit makes a plan to get the ox without waiting for his work to be finished. However, he sees the tiger returning and decides to stay and watch him until the farm is cleared. This event resolves the previous conflict of the rabbit wanting to leave and the tiger's son watching over him.

events.description: Then the tiger gave the rabbit the ox as he had promised. “You must kill this ox,” he said to the rabbit, “in a place where there are neither flies nor mosquitoes.”

events.description: There would be flies, of course. He went on farther and again he thought that he would kill the ox. The ground looked moist and damp and so did the leaves on the bushes. Since the rabbit thought there would be mosquitoes there he decided not to kill the ox.

events.name: Tiger Jumps Stockade Walls
events.description: He thought what good hunting there was in the jungle. He thought how cool the water was at the spring. Once more he jumped and jumped with all his might at the back side of the stockade.
thoughts: The tiger's actions are part of his attempt to escape the stockade built by the rabbit. This event is a continuation of the previous events where the tiger tries various methods to break free. The story has reached its mid-point and the narrative is still building up towards the climax.

The rabbit has already built a stockade around himself and now the tiger asks for help. The tiger's attempts to break out are unsuccessful, which indicates that the event is part of the main narrative action: it drives the story forward through conflict.

The tiger's actions are part of his attempt to escape from the stockade built by the rabbit. This event is a continuation of the previous events where the tiger tries to get out of the stockade but fails. It's not a climactic moment that resolves an open conflict, nor is it a key action driving the story forward.

was deceived by the rabbit's request for help

events.name: Tiger Breaks Through Stockade
events.description: At last he broke through. He did not get through, however, without getting bad cuts on both his sides. Until this day the tiger has stripes on both his sides.
thoughts: The event where the tiger breaks through the stockade and gets injured is a turning point in the story. The rabbit's clever plan has been executed, and the tiger is now trapped and unable to escape. This event marks a shift from the setup and escalation phases of the narrative to the climax.

The tiger's attempt to break through the stockade built by the rabbit is a pivotal moment in the story. It represents a turning point where the tiger, who had previously outsmarted and deceived others, is now trapped and unable to escape. This event marks a shift from the tiger's dominance to his vulnerability.

The tiger's attempt to break through the stockade built by the rabbit is a turning point in their ongoing conflict. The tiger has been outsmarted and trapped, and this event marks a shift towards his eventual defeat.
